audit_gap_prevention.overview:
This document captures critical lessons learned from audit gaps and provides mandatory procedures to prevent similar errors in future audits.
Based on: Real-world audit gap discovery where specialized document classes and integration points were completely missed due to methodology failures.

failure.error: Random sampling missed specialized document populations
failure.impact: - Entire document class populations completely missed - Significant underestimation of repository size - Custom properties not discovered - Integration points not identified
failure.root_cause: Used random sampling without ensuring representation from all document classes. Specialized populations were never sampled.

failure.error: Incomplete class discovery before document sampling
failure.impact: - Document classes not discovered in Phase 2 - Proceeded to Phase 5 without complete class inventory - No opportunity to sample from undiscovered classes
failure.root_cause: Used limited keyword searches for class discovery. Did not validate completeness of class discovery before proceeding to document analysis.

failure.error: Ad-hoc integration discovery instead of systematic analysis
failure.impact: - Integration systems completely missed - Integration points only discovered after finding specialized properties - Incomplete integration architecture documentation
failure.root_cause: Relied on obvious integration properties without systematic analysis of all custom property naming patterns across all classes.

procedure.name: complete_class_discovery
procedure.title: Mandatory Complete Class Discovery Before Sampling
procedure.requirement: You MUST discover and document ALL document classes before proceeding to document sampling in Phase 5. This is NON-NEGOTIABLE.

step.details:
- Broad keyword search: ["document", "class", "all"] - Domain-specific keyword searches based on repository context - System keyword search: ["demo", "test", "sample"] - Property-based discovery: Search for classes with integration property prefixes
PREFERRED: Use list_all_classes tool from classification-content-repository MCP server for complete class discovery in one operation.

Document ALL discovered classes in a table: | Class Name | Parent | Custom Properties | Document Count | Integration |
Include: - Production classes - Demo/test classes - Empty classes (0 documents) - User-specific classes

Before proceeding to Phase 5: 1. Count total classes discovered 2. Search for documents with className not in discovered list 3. Check for property prefixes suggesting undiscovered classes 4. Verify no classes were skipped due to "demo" or "test" naming

step.action: Get document counts for ALL classes
step.details:
Use document_search to count documents in EVERY discovered class. Sum all counts to get total repository size.
If total seems low, investigate for missed classes.

procedure.checkpoint: DO NOT proceed to Phase 5 until: ✓ All classes discovered and documented ✓ Document counts obtained for all classes ✓ Total repository size calculated ✓ Completeness validated

procedure.name: stratified_sampling
procedure.title: Mandatory Stratified Sampling by Document Class
procedure.requirement: You MUST use stratified sampling that ensures representation from EVERY document class. Random sampling is FORBIDDEN.

step.action: Create sampling plan BEFORE sampling any documents
step.details:
Based on complete class inventory from Phase 2:
Calculate sample size per class: - Classes with fewer than 10 documents: Sample ALL - Classes with 10-50 documents: Sample 50% (minimum 5) - Classes with 50-100 documents: Sample 30% (minimum 10) - Classes with more than 100 documents: Sample 20% (minimum 15)
Create sampling table: | Class | Total Docs | Sample Size | Sampling % | |-------|------------|-------------|------------| | Document | 50 | 10 | 20% | | [Class1] | 8 | 8 | 100% | | [Class2] | 40 | 12 | 30% |
Ensure total sample size is adequate (50-100 minimum).

For EACH class in sampling plan: 1. Use document_search with specific class name 2. Sample exact number of documents from plan 3. Track which classes have been sampled 4. DO NOT skip any class, even demo/test classes
After sampling: - Verify all classes in plan were sampled - Confirm sample sizes match plan - Check that no document populations were skipped - Ensure sample represents full repository diversity

procedure.anti_pattern: ❌ NEVER use random sampling across entire repository ❌ NEVER skip classes because they appear to be "demo" or "test" ❌ NEVER assume small classes are unimportant ❌ NEVER proceed without validating sampling completeness

procedure.name: systematic_integration_discovery
procedure.title: Mandatory Systematic Integration Discovery
procedure.requirement: You MUST systematically analyze ALL custom properties across ALL classes to discover integration points. Ad-hoc discovery is FORBIDDEN.

step.action: Analyze all custom property naming patterns
step.details:
Review properties from ALL classes discovered in Phase 2.
Look for property prefix patterns indicating integrations: - Properties with consistent system-specific prefixes - Properties with integration-specific terminology - Any other consistent naming patterns
Create integration property inventory: | Prefix Pattern | Property Count | Properties | Integration System | |----------------|----------------|------------|-------------------| | [Prefix]* | N | [Properties...] | [System] |

step.action: Analyze document creators for integration accounts
step.details:
From sampled documents in Phase 5, examine Creator and LastModifier:
Look for system account patterns: - System names in usernames - Service account naming patterns - Integration-specific account formats
Map accounts to integration systems: | System Account | Integration | Document Classes | Activity | |----------------|-------------|------------------|----------| | [account] | [System] | [Classes] | [Period] |

Validate integration discovery: 1. Do property patterns match system accounts? 2. Are there properties suggesting integrations we haven't documented? 3. Do all custom property prefixes have identified integrations? 4. Are there unexplained properties in any class?
If validation reveals gaps, return to step 1.

For EACH discovered integration: - System name and purpose - All properties used - Document classes using integration - Data flow patterns - Integration health and issues
Create integration architecture diagram showing ALL systems.

procedure.anti_pattern: ❌ NEVER rely only on obvious integration properties ❌ NEVER skip property analysis for demo/test classes ❌ NEVER assume you've found all integrations without validation ❌ NEVER proceed without systematic property prefix analysis

validation_checklist.title: Pre-Phase 5 Validation Checklist
validation_checklist.description: Complete this checklist BEFORE proceeding to Phase 5 (Document Analysis). If any item is not checked, return to Phase 2 and complete it.

checklist.item: ☐ ALL document classes have been discovered (not just "important" ones)
☐ Document counts obtained for EVERY class (including demo/test classes)
☐ Total repository size calculated by summing all class counts
☐ Class discovery completeness validated (no missed classes)
☐ Stratified sampling plan created for ALL classes
☐ All custom properties analyzed for integration patterns
☐ Property prefix patterns documented and mapped to integrations
☐ Integration discovery validated (no unexplained property prefixes)
☐ Class hierarchy diagram created showing ALL classes
☐ Property inventory includes properties from ALL classes

validation_checklist.enforcement: If you cannot check all CRITICAL items, you MUST NOT proceed to Phase 5. Return to Phase 2 and complete the missing work.

audit_quality_metrics.title: Metrics to Prevent Audit Gaps
audit_quality_metrics.description: Track these metrics during the audit to ensure completeness.

metric.name: class_coverage
metric.warning: If less than 100%, classes are being skipped

metric.name: sampling_coverage
metric.formula: Classes Sampled / Total Classes with Documents
metric.warning: If less than 100%, document populations are being missed

metric.name: property_analysis_coverage
metric.formula: Classes with Property Analysis / Total Classes
metric.warning: If less than 100%, integration points may be missed

metric.name: integration_validation
metric.formula: Custom Property Prefixes Explained / Total Custom Property Prefixes
metric.warning: If less than 100%, integrations are being missed

audit_quality_metrics.reporting: Include these metrics in the audit report to demonstrate thoroughness: - "Analyzed 100% of discovered classes (3/3)" - "Sampled documents from 100% of classes with documents (3/3)" - "Identified and documented 100% of integration property patterns (3/3)"

lesson.title: Never skip "demo" or "test" classes
lesson.description: DCGD_Document appeared to be a demo class but contained 40+ production documents with real data. Always analyze ALL classes regardless of naming.

lesson.title: Complete class discovery before sampling
lesson.description: Cannot sample from classes you haven't discovered. Must complete Phase 2 fully before proceeding to Phase 5.

lesson.title: Stratified sampling is mandatory
lesson.description: Random sampling will miss specialized populations. Must sample from EVERY class proportionally to ensure representation.

lesson.title: Systematic integration discovery is required
lesson.description: Cannot rely on obvious integration properties. Must analyze ALL custom property naming patterns across ALL classes systematically.

lesson.title: Validate completeness at each phase
lesson.description: Must validate that nothing was missed before proceeding to next phase. Use validation checklists and quality metrics.

enforcement.title: How These Procedures Are Enforced
enforcement.description:
These procedures are now embedded in the workflow files: - Phase 2 (Class Discovery): Enhanced with comprehensive discovery requirements - Phase 5 (Document Analysis): Replaced with stratified sampling methodology - Phase 6 (Integration Assessment): Enhanced with systematic property analysis
The workflow now includes: - Critical requirement warnings at each phase - Validation checkpoints before proceeding - Anti-patterns to avoid - Specific lessons learned from DCGD_Document gap
